title: Clyde Hydrogen Produces Hydrogen from First Fully Integrated Prototype System in Milestone Breakthrough
content: Clyde Hydrogen Systems, a University of Glasgow spin-out, has successfully completed and produced hydrogen from its first fully integrated prototype system. This milestone validates the companyâ€™s proprietary decoupled electrolysis technology, which allows for the direct connection to renewable power and the storage of hydrogen in a non-gaseous form. Designed to be safer, simpler, and more cost-effective than conventional alternatives, the system addresses key barriers in long-term energy storage. Following a technical breakthrough last year where it produced high-pressure hydrogen, Clyde Hydrogen is now focusing on scaling up to a commercial demonstrator, with a market-ready product targeted for 2028. The company is currently seeking to raise up to Â£5 million in seed funding by the second quarter of 2026 to support commercial development and team expansion. Backers include Zinc, the University of Glasgow, and Ecotricity.
author: Analysts Account
